description: Occupied hospitals require negative pressure isolation rooms for airborne infection control per HCAI requirements.
violation_template: {occupancy} project includes isolation rooms. Verify negative pressure design (minimum -0.01 in. w.g.) is shown on drawings with pressure differential monitoring and visual indicators at room entry.
fix_template: Provide pressure differential diagram on mechanical drawings. Show monitoring device location and alarm setpoints per Title 24 Part 4 and ASHRAE 170.

description: Operating rooms and procedure rooms require positive pressure with minimum air changes per hour (ACH) and HEPA filtration.
violation_template: Operating/procedure rooms in {occupancy} must maintain positive pressure relative to adjacent corridors with minimum 20 ACH total (4 ACH outside air). HEPA filtration (99.97% at 0.3 micron) is required.
fix_template:
1. Revise mechanical schedules to confirm 20 ACH minimum (4 ACH OA) for OR/procedure rooms.
2. Provide HEPA filter schedule with efficiency rating.
3. Show pressure relationship diagram indicating positive pressure to adjacent spaces.

description: California Seismic Zone D/E hospitals (Ip=1.5) require OSHPD-preapproved seismic anchorage for medical equipment and nonstructural components.
violation_template: Project is in Seismic Zone {seismic_zone}. All nonstructural components (HVAC units, med gas equipment, electrical distribution) must be anchored per CBC Chapter 13 with OSHPD preapproved hardware.
fix_template:
1. Provide seismic anchorage calculations for all equipment >400 lbs or vibration-producing equipment.
2. Specify OSHPD pre-approved anchorage manufacturer and model.
3. Show anchorage details on structural drawings referencing CBC 2022 Chapter 13.

description: Hospitals must have a code-compliant Essential Electrical System (EES) with separated Critical, Life Safety, and Equipment branches.
violation_template: Essential Electrical System for {occupancy} must comply with NFPA 99-2021 Chapter 6 and Title 24 Part 3. Drawings must show three separate EES branches: Life Safety, Critical, and Equipment.
fix_template:
1. Provide single-line diagram clearly labeled with Life Safety, Critical, and Equipment branch separations.
2. Show transfer time ≤10 seconds for Life Safety branch per NFPA 99.
3. Label all receptacles and equipment served by each EES branch on electrical plans.

description: Medical gas systems including oxygen, vacuum, medical air, and WAGD require NFPA 99 compliance with zone valves and master alarm panels.
violation_template: Medical gas systems in {occupancy} must comply with NFPA 99-2021 Chapter 5. Zone valve boxes (ZVBs), master alarm panels (MAPs), and area alarm panels must be shown on drawings.
fix_template:
1. Provide medical gas system riser diagram with ZVB locations.
2. Show MAP location accessible from nursing station.
3. Provide area alarm panel locations per NFPA 99 Section 5.1.9.
4. Note gas outlet types and quantities per room schedule.

description: Healthcare facilities require ASSE 1070 thermostatic mixing valves at patient-use fixtures to prevent Legionella and scalding.
violation_template: Patient-accessible lavatories and showers in {occupancy} require ASSE 1070-compliant thermostatic mixing valves with 110°F maximum delivery temperature to prevent scalding and Legionella growth.
fix_template:
1. Specify ASSE 1070 mixing valves at all patient-accessible fixtures.
2. Set domestic hot water supply at 140°F minimum (Legionella control) with point-of-use tempering to 110°F max.
3. Provide water temperature management plan per Title 22 CCR.

description: ICU patient rooms require minimum 6 ACH outside air and controlled pressure relationships per ASHRAE 170.
violation_template: ICU/NICU patient rooms in {occupancy} require minimum 6 ACH outside air with positive pressure to corridor per ASHRAE 170 Table 7.1. Current drawings do not confirm compliance.
fix_template:
1. Update mechanical schedules to show 6 ACH OA minimum for ICU rooms.
2. Provide pressure relationship diagram showing positive pressure (+0.01 in. w.g. min).
3. Confirm 100% exhaust — no air recirculation from ICU patient rooms.

description: Hospital patient corridors must maintain minimum 8-foot clear width for emergency egress and equipment movement.
violation_template: Patient care corridors in {occupancy} must maintain minimum 8-foot (96-inch) clear width per CBC Section 1005.1 Exception 5 and Title 24 Part 2 Section 1224.1. Verify corridor widths are unobstructed.
fix_template:
1. Dimension all patient care corridors on architectural plans.
2. Show 8-foot minimum clear width (96 inches) unobstructed.
3. Coordinate medical equipment alcoves and nurse stations to maintain required clear width.

description: Patient rooms, restrooms, and public areas must comply with CBC Chapter 11B accessibility requirements.
violation_template: Patient rooms and associated toilet rooms must comply with CBC Chapter 11B accessibility requirements including turning radius, grab bars, knee clearances, and door hardware.
fix_template:
1. Provide accessibility compliance notes on architectural drawings referencing CBC Chapter 11B.
2. Show 60-inch turning diameter in patient toilet rooms.
3. Dimension grab bar locations and heights (33"-36" AFF).
4. Specify lever door hardware throughout accessible routes.

description: Scrub sinks adjacent to ORs must be hands-free operable and meet minimum quantity requirements per FGI Guidelines.
violation_template: Scrub sink stations adjacent to operating/procedure rooms in {occupancy} must be hands-free (knee or sensor-actuated) with minimum one scrub station per OR per FGI Guidelines 2018.
fix_template:
1. Specify hands-free scrub sinks (knee/sensor actuated) at each OR.
2. Locate scrub sinks adjacent to OR entry — confirm on floor plans.
3. Provide minimum one scrub station per operating room.
4. Note ASSE 1070 compliance for water temperature.

description: Suspended ceilings in patient care areas must be seismically restrained per OSHPD requirements.
violation_template: Suspended ceiling systems in patient care areas of {occupancy} (Seismic Zone {seismic_zone}) require seismic restraint per CBC Chapter 25 and OSHPD-preapproved ceiling system documentation.
fix_template:
1. Specify OSHPD pre-approved seismic ceiling system (e.g., USG Donn DX or equivalent).
2. Provide ceiling suspension details with seismic bracing per CBC Chapter 25.
3. Show hanger wire spacing, perimeter angle, and seismic bracing on reflected ceiling plans.

description: Smoke compartmentalization is required in hospitals with corridor smoke barriers at maximum 200-foot intervals.
violation_template: Smoke barriers in {occupancy} must divide the building into smoke compartments not exceeding 22,500 sq ft with maximum 200-foot travel distance per CBC Section 407.4 and Title 24 Part 2.
fix_template:
1. Show smoke barrier locations on all floor plans.
2. Dimension smoke compartments — confirm ≤22,500 sq ft and ≤200 ft travel distance.
3. Specify smoke-rated door assemblies (S-rated) at smoke barrier openings.
4. Show smoke damper locations at HVAC penetrations of smoke barriers.

description: Sterile Processing Departments (SPD) require a minimum of 4 ACH outside air with negative pressure and specific air distribution.
violation_template: Sterile Processing / Soiled Utility areas in {occupancy} require negative pressure with minimum 10 ACH total air changes. Decontamination area must be at negative pressure relative to clean areas.
fix_template:
1. Show negative pressure in decontamination/soiled areas relative to clean/sterile areas.
2. Confirm 10 ACH minimum total air changes in mechanical schedules.
3. Provide pressure relationship diagram showing airflow direction from clean to soiled.

description: Hospital pharmacies with compounding areas require ISO Class 7 cleanroom conditions with HEPA filtration.
violation_template: Pharmacy compounding areas in {occupancy} require ISO Class 7 cleanroom environment with HEPA-filtered supply air, positive pressure, and 30 ACH minimum per USP <797> and ASHRAE 170.
fix_template:
1. Provide ISO Class 7 cleanroom specifications for pharmacy compounding room.
2. Show HEPA filters (99.97% efficiency) in supply air system.
3. Confirm positive pressure relative to adjacent areas.
4. Note 30 ACH minimum in mechanical schedule.
5. Reference USP <797> and <800> compliance on drawings.

description: Patient care areas require redundant branch circuits with isolated ground receptacles and GFCI protection per NFPA 99.
violation_template: Patient care areas in {occupancy} require a minimum of two individual circuits from separate branch panels per NFPA 99. Isolated ground receptacles (orange face) are required in wet locations (ORs, procedure rooms).
fix_template:
1. Show minimum two branch circuits from separate panels at each patient bed.
2. Specify isolated ground receptacles (IG) in OR and procedure rooms.
3. Label receptacle types and circuit sources on electrical plans.
4. Coordinate with EES branch requirements per NFPA 99 Chapter 6.

description: Type V (wood-frame) and Type III construction is prohibited for OSHPD-1 acute care occupancies above one story per California Building Code.
violation_template: {construction_type} construction is not permitted for {occupancy} facilities subject to OSHPD-1 review. California Health and Safety Code Section 1250 requires non-combustible (Type I or Type II) construction for licensed general acute care hospitals.
fix_template:
1. Revise construction type to Type I-A, I-B, II-A, or II-B on the cover sheet and applicable code analysis sheets.
2. Provide a structural system narrative demonstrating non-combustible framing throughout.
3. Re-submit code analysis table showing compliance with CBC Section 504 height and area limits for the revised construction type.
code_references: California Health and Safety Code Section 1250

description: Hospitals with 100 or more licensed beds must meet IBC Risk Category IV seismic performance requirements with enhanced inspections and special inspections program.
violation_template: This {licensed_beds}-bed {occupancy} facility exceeds the 100-bed threshold requiring IBC Risk Category IV structural performance. Verify that the structural design criteria, drift limits, and redundancy factors satisfy Risk Category IV per ASCE 7-22.
fix_template:
1. Update structural calculations cover sheet to declare IBC Risk Category IV.
2. Revise seismic drift limits to h/100 (ASCE 7-22 Table 12.12-1, Risk Category IV).
3. Prepare Special Inspections Program per CBC Chapter 17 covering moment frames, shear walls, anchor bolts, and welding.
4. Confirm redundancy factor ρ = 1.3 is applied unless qualifying exception is documented.

description: Type I-A and I-B construction requires all structural members and exterior wall assemblies to have a minimum 2-hour fire-resistance rating with no combustible concealed spaces.
violation_template: {construction_type} construction requires all primary structural members (columns, beams, bearing walls) and exterior walls to carry a minimum 2-hour fire-resistance rating per CBC Table 601. Verify fire-resistance assemblies are listed in the UL Directory or equivalent.
fix_template:
1. Provide a fire-resistance assembly schedule on architectural drawings referencing UL listing numbers or CBC Table 720.
2. Ensure floor-ceiling assemblies match the 2-hour rating; address any through-penetrations with listed firestop systems.
3. Show no combustible concealed spaces; where unavoidable, provide automatic sprinkler protection per NFPA 13 and note on drawings.

description: Non-sprinklered healthcare occupancies must comply with CBC Table 1004.5 reduced occupant load factors and more restrictive travel distance and egress width requirements.
violation_template: {occupancy} is indicated as non-sprinklered. Verify that egress travel distances comply with the more restrictive non-sprinklered limits per CBC Table 1017.2 (exit access travel distance ≤150 ft for Group I-2) and that egress widths and occupant loads reflect the non-sprinklered factors in CBC Table 1004.5.
fix_template:
1. Confirm facility is non-sprinklered on the code summary sheet; if sprinklers are present update sprinkler status.
2. Verify exit access travel distance ≤150 ft (non-sprinklered limit for I-2) on all floor plans.
3. Recalculate occupant load using non-sprinklered factors from CBC Table 1004.5.
4. Show egress width calculations (0.3 in/occupant for corridors, 0.2 in/occupant for doors) per CBC Section 1005.

description: Sprinklered healthcare facilities must provide a complete NFPA 13 sprinkler system schedule and hydraulic calculations stamped by a licensed fire protection engineer.
violation_template: {occupancy} is indicated as fully sprinklered (NFPA 13). Verify that the fire sprinkler drawings include: a complete sprinkler head schedule with K-factors and temperature ratings, hydraulic calculation summary, and riser diagram. All fire sprinkler work must bear the stamp of a California-licensed Fire Protection Engineer or C-16 contractor.
fix_template:
1. Provide a sprinkler head schedule on fire protection drawings including head type, K-factor, temperature rating, and coverage area.
2. Include hydraulic calculation summary table (design area, density, residual pressure, flow demand).
3. Show the riser diagram with backflow preventer, OS&Y valve, flow switch, and inspector's test connection.
4. Confirm drawings bear the stamp of a licensed Fire Protection Engineer (PE) or C-16 contractor as required by California Health and Safety Code Section 13145.
code_references: California Health and Safety Code Section 13145

description: Buildings exceeding 55 ft in height (OSHPD high-rise threshold) must comply with CBC Section 403 high-rise provisions including a fire command center, voice/alarm communication system, and fire service access elevator.
violation_template: This {height_ft}-ft {occupancy} exceeds the 55-ft OSHPD high-rise threshold. CBC Section 403 provisions apply: a fire command center, an approved voice/alarm communication system, and at least one fire service access elevator must be provided and shown on the drawings.
fix_template:
1. Designate a fire command center on the ground floor per CBC Section 403.4.6; show location and equipment list.
2. Design and document a voice/alarm communication system (two-way) per CBC Section 403.4.4 covering all floors.
3. Provide at least one fire service access elevator per CBC Section 403.6; show hoistway dimensions and recall panel location.
4. Confirm sprinkler system extends to all floors including mechanical penthouses per NFPA 13.
5. Coordinate high-rise provisions checklist with HCAI plan reviewer at pre-application meeting.

description: Buildings of four or more stories above grade with healthcare occupancies require a stairway pressurization system (smoke-proof enclosures) per CBC Section 909.20.
violation_template: This {stories}-story {occupancy} requires stair enclosures to be designed as smoke-proof enclosures per CBC Section 909.20. Natural ventilation, mechanical pressurization, or vestibule approach must be shown on mechanical and architectural drawings.
fix_template:
1. Select a smoke-proof enclosure approach (natural ventilation, mechanical pressurization at ≥0.05 in. w.g., or vestibule) per CBC Section 909.20.
2. Show stair vestibule dimensions and door hardware (self-closing, positive-latch) on architectural plans.
3. If mechanical pressurization: provide fan schedule, ductwork layout, and controls sequence on mechanical drawings.
4. Coordinate smoke-proof enclosure design with the overall smoke control/management plan per CBC Section 909.

description: Los Angeles County local amendment requires peer review of seismic design for OSHPD-1 hospitals by a county-approved structural engineer in addition to HCAI review.
violation_template: Project is located in {county} County. LA County Building and Safety requires an independent peer review of the lateral force-resisting system by an LA County pre-qualified structural engineer, in addition to standard HCAI review. Confirm peer review engagement and include peer reviewer's acceptance letter with the permit application.
fix_template:
1. Retain an LA County pre-qualified structural engineer for peer review per LA County Code Section 110.3.3.
2. Provide the peer reviewer with the full structural package (calculations, drawings, geotechnical report).
3. Incorporate all peer review comments before HCAI final submittal.
4. Include the peer reviewer's acceptance letter and comment/response log in the permit application.
